audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra grain offering text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for grain/minhah offering, oil, frankincense, memorial portion, priestly portion, no leaven/honey, salt of covenant, firstfruits.

verses.restored: When a soul/life brings near grain offering to YHWH, his offering shall be fine flour; he shall pour oil upon it and put frankincense upon it.

He shall bring it to sons of Aaron, the priests; he shall grasp from there full handful from its fine flour and oil with all its frankincense, and priest shall turn its memorial portion into smoke on altar, fire-offering, soothing scent to YHWH.
The remainder of grain offering belongs to Aaron and his sons, Holy of Holies from fire-offerings of YHWH.
When you bring near offering of grain offering baked in oven, it shall be unleavened cakes of fine flour mixed with oil or unleavened wafers spread with oil.
If your offering is grain offering on griddle, it shall be fine flour mixed with oil, unleavened.
Break it into pieces and pour oil upon it; it is grain offering.
If your offering is grain offering in pan, it shall be made of fine flour with oil.
You shall bring grain offering that is made of these to YHWH; it shall be brought near to priest, and he shall bring it to altar.
The priest shall lift from grain offering its memorial portion and turn it into smoke on altar, fire-offering, soothing scent to YHWH.
The remainder of grain offering belongs to Aaron and his sons, Holy of Holies from fire-offerings of YHWH.
Every grain offering you bring near to YHWH shall not be made leavened, for any leaven or any honey you shall not turn into smoke as fire-offering to YHWH.
As offering of firstfruits you may bring them near to YHWH, but they shall not go up on altar for soothing scent.

Every offering of your grain offering you shall season with salt; you shall not let salt of covenant of your Divine be lacking from your grain offering. With all your offerings you shall bring near salt.
If you bring near grain offering of firstfruits to YHWH, fresh ears roasted with fire, crushed grain of fresh grain, you shall bring near as grain offering of your firstfruits.
You shall put oil upon it and place frankincense upon it; it is grain offering.
The priest shall turn its memorial portion into smoke, from its crushed grain and from its oil, with all its frankincense; fire-offering to YHWH.

translation_notes: Minhah/grain offering is non-animal offering and should be kept visible as valid near-bringing.
Memorial portion/azkarah rises while remainder feeds priests; offering includes shared provision.
Salt of covenant preserves durability/faithfulness symbolism.
Leaven/honey restriction is ritual-specific and not a blanket negative theology of sweetness or fermentation.
safety_note: Leviticus 2 prevents reducing sacrifice to animal blood. Grain, oil, fragrance, salt, and firstfruits are also holy modes of drawing near.
